title: erucastrum varium f. campestre
intro: Erucastrum varium f. campestre, a subspecies of the Erucastrum varium plant, is a type of flowering plant in the mustard family (Brassicaceae). It is also known as common dog's tooth or common dogtooth grass. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The leaves of the plant can be used to make herbal preparations such as teas and infusions, which are believed to have health benefits including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting respiratory health.
